children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to provide for a center for the interpretation of the Siege and Battle of Corinth and other Civil War actions in the Region and to enhance public understanding of the significance of the Corinth Campaign in the Civil War relative to the Western theater of operations, in cooperation with State or local governmental entities and private organizations and individuals.

children.heading: Acquisition of property at Corinth, Mississippi
children.label: b Acquisition of property at Corinth, Mississippi
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior (referred to in this title as the “Secretary”) shall acquire by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange, such land and interests in land in the vicinity of the Corinth Battlefield, in the State of Mississippi, as the Secretary determines to be necessary for the construction of an interpretive center to commemorate and interpret the 1862 Civil War Siege and Battle of Corinth.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Publicly owned land
children.label: c Publicly owned land
children.text: Land and interests in land owned by the State of Mississippi or a political subdivision of the State of Mississippi may be acquired only by donation.

children.heading: Interpretive center
children.label: 1 Interpretive center
children.text: The Secretary shall construct, operate, and maintain on the property acquired under subsection (b) a center for the interpretation of the Siege and Battle of Corinth and associated historical events for the benefit of the public.

children.heading: Marking
children.label: 2 Marking
children.text: The Secretary may mark sites associated with the Siege and Battle of Corinth National Historic Landmark, as designated on , if the sites are determined by the Secretary to be protected by State or local governmental agencies.May 6, 1991

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 3 Administration
children.text: The land and interests in land acquired, and the facilities constructed and maintained pursuant to this section, shall be administered by the Secretary as a part of Shiloh National Military Park, subject to the appropriate laws (including regulations) applicable to the Park, the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved ( et seq.), and the Act entitled “An Act to provide for the preservation of historic American sites, buildings, objects, and antiquities of national significance, and for other purposes”, approved ( et seq.).August 25, 1916August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 116 U.S.C. 46111

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $6,000,000 for development to carry out this section.
